raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "The statement serves as a directive or principle prescribing a specific spiritual practice for aligning one's will with a higher power, and it functions as guidance for personal transformation within a recovery or spiritual context.",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "There exists a God or higher power with whom conscious contact can be improved.",
    "This God has a will for individuals that can be known.",
    "Prayer and meditation are effective means to improve contact with this God.",
    "Knowing God's will and having power to carry it out are desirable and attainable.",
    "The individual's understanding of God is subjective and permissible ('as we understood Him')."
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "The statement is internally coherent within its own spiritual framework: it proposes a means (prayer and meditation) to achieve a specific end (improved conscious contact) with a defined purpose (seeking knowledge of God's will and power to act). The phrasing is self-consistent.",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": [
    "There is a shared belief in a higher power or God, however defined, that is responsive to prayer and meditation.",
    "Individuals are willing to engage in prayer and meditation as genuine practices.",
    "The goal of aligning with a divine will is valued by the community or system.",
    "The concept of 'conscious contact' is considered meaningful and achievable."
  ],
  "conditions_for_misleading": [
    "If no such God or higher power exists, the statement may mislead people into pursuing an unachievable goal.",
    "If prayer and meditation are ineffective for improving contact with any deity, the statement could foster false hope or dependency.",
    "If the statement is applied to individuals who do not share theistic beliefs, it may be coercive or irrelevant.",
    "If the statement is taken literally as a universal principle without acknowledging alternative spiritual or secular approaches."
  ]
}

parsed_json.communicative_function: The statement serves as a directive or principle prescribing a specific spiritual practice for aligning one's will with a higher power, and it functions as guidance for personal transformation within a recovery or spiritual context.
implicit_claims: There exists a God or higher power with whom conscious contact can be improved.
This God has a will for individuals that can be known.
Prayer and meditation are effective means to improve contact with this God.
Knowing God's will and having power to carry it out are desirable and attainable.
The individual's understanding of God is subjective and permissible ('as we understood Him').
parsed_json.coherence_assessment: The statement is internally coherent within its own spiritual framework: it proposes a means (prayer and meditation) to achieve a specific end (improved conscious contact) with a defined purpose (seeking knowledge of God's will and power to act). The phrasing is self-consistent.
conditions_for_usefulness: There is a shared belief in a higher power or God, however defined, that is responsive to prayer and meditation.
Individuals are willing to engage in prayer and meditation as genuine practices.
The goal of aligning with a divine will is valued by the community or system.
The concept of 'conscious contact' is considered meaningful and achievable.
conditions_for_misleading: If no such God or higher power exists, the statement may mislead people into pursuing an unachievable goal.
If prayer and meditation are ineffective for improving contact with any deity, the statement could foster false hope or dependency.
If the statement is applied to individuals who do not share theistic beliefs, it may be coercive or irrelevant.
If the statement is taken literally as a universal principle without acknowledging alternative spiritual or secular approaches.
